title: ब्रह्मा चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
ब्रह्मा देव सृजनहार, कमलासन भगवान।
चालीसा कहूँ भक्ति से, दो ज्ञान वरदान॥
चार मुख चार वेद के, ज्ञाता परम प्रवीण।
ब्रह्मा जी की वन्दना, करूँ हृदय से लीन॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय ब्रह्मा देव सृजनहारी।
जगत रचना के तुम अधिकारी॥
कमल से प्रकटे विष्णु नाभि से।
सृष्टि रची तुमने अपने रभि से॥
चार मुख हैं तुम्हरे सुविशाला।
चार वेद मुख से निकले माला॥
ऋग्वेद यजुर्वेद सामा अथर्वा।
चार वेद तव मुख से सर्वा॥
सावित्री शक्ति तव पत्नी प्यारी।
सरस्वती विद्या की अधिकारी॥
हंसवाहन पर सदा विराजो।
श्वेत वस्त्र कमण्डलु साजो॥
रुद्राक्षमाला कर में शोभे।
वेदपुस्तक हस्त सब लोभे॥
सृष्टि के आदि प्रजापति।
सब जीवों के तुम्हीं हो अधिपति॥
चौदह भुवन रचे तुमने भारी।
स्वर्ग पाताल और मृत्यु संसारी॥
मरीचि अत्रि अंगिरा पुलस्त्य।
पुलह क्रतु सात ऋषि तव वत्स्य॥
दक्ष प्रजापति तव पुत्र कहाये।
मनु शतरूपा तव मानस आये॥
काल रचना तव कृति विशेषा।
युग कल्प मन्वन्तर ये लेखा॥
ब्रह्मलोक तव धाम सुहाना।
सत्यलोक सबसे ऊपर जाना॥
ब्रह्मा वैष्णव शैव तिहारी।
त्रिमूर्ति में प्रथम अधिकारी॥
सृष्टि पालन संहार तीनों।
ब्रह्मा विष्णु महेश प्रवीणों॥
पुष्कर क्षेत्र तव मन्दिर भारी।
एकमात्र ब्रह्मा मन्दिर न्यारी॥
कर्मकाण्ड के तुम आधारा।
यज्ञविधि तुमने रची अपारा॥
वेद शास्त्र पुराण तुम भाषे।
स्मृति उपनिषद तव ज्ञान प्रकाशे॥
गायत्री मन्त्र तव शक्ति महाना।
सावित्री व्रत तव कथा सुजाना॥
सृजन शक्ति के स्वामी तुम्हीं हो।
ज्ञान विज्ञान के धामी तुम्हीं हो॥
जो ब्रह्मा का ध्यान लगावे।
सृजनशीलता वरदान पावे॥
विद्या बुद्धि ज्ञान का दाता।
ब्रह्मा देव सृष्टि के त्राता॥
नित्य चालीसा जो कोई गावे।
ब्रह्मा जी की कृपा पावे॥

॥ दोहा ॥
ब्रह्मा चालीसा पढ़े, ज्ञान भक्ति मन लाय।
सृजनशक्ति बुद्धि बढ़े, सब विद्या फल पाय॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Brahma Dev Srijanhaar, Kamalasan Bhagwan.
Chalisa Kahun Bhakti Se, Do Jnan Vardan.
Char Mukh Char Ved Ke, Jnata Param Praveen.
Brahma Ji Ki Vandana, Karun Hriday Se Leen.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Brahma Dev Srijnahari.
Jagat Rachna Ke Tum Adhikari.
Kamal Se Prakte Vishnu Nabhi Se.
Srishti Rachi Tumne Apne Rabhi Se.
Char Mukh Hain Tumhre Suvishala.
Char Ved Mukh Se Nikle Mala.
Rigved Yajurved Sama Atharva.
Char Ved Tav Mukh Se Sarva.
Savitri Shakti Tav Patni Pyari.
Saraswati Vidya Ki Adhikari.
Hansavahan Par Sada Virajo.
Swet Vastra Kamandalu Sajo.
Rudraksha Mala Kar Mein Shobhe.
Ved Pustak Hast Sab Lobhe.
Srishti Ke Aadi Prajapati.
Sab Jeevon Ke Tumhi Ho Adhipati.
Chaudah Bhuvan Rache Tumne Bhari.
Swarg Patal Aur Mrityu Sansari.
Marichi Atri Angira Pulastya.
Pulah Kratu Saat Rishi Tav Vatsya.
Daksh Prajapati Tav Putra Kahaye.
Manu Shatarupa Tav Manas Aaye.
Kal Rachna Tav Kriti Vishesha.
Yug Kalp Manvantar Ye Lekha.
Brahmalok Tav Dham Suhana.
Satylok Sabse Upar Jana.
Brahma Vaishnav Shaiv Tihari.
Trimurti Mein Pratham Adhikari.
Srishti Palan Sanhar Teenon.
Brahma Vishnu Mahesh Praveenon.
Pushkar Kshetra Tav Mandir Bhari.
Ekmatra Brahma Mandir Nyari.
Karmkand Ke Tum Adhara.
Yajnavidhi Tumne Rachi Apara.
Ved Shastra Puran Tum Bhashe.
Smriti Upanishad Tav Jnan Prakashe.
Gayatri Mantra Tav Shakti Mahana.
Savitri Vrat Tav Katha Sujana.
Srijan Shakti Ke Swami Tumhi Ho.
Jnan Vigyan Ke Dhami Tumhi Ho.
Jo Brahma Ka Dhyan Lagave.
Srijanshilta Vardan Pave.
Vidya Buddhi Jnan Ka Data.
Brahma Dev Srishti Ke Trata.
Nitya Chalisa Jo Koi Gave.
Brahma Ji Ki Kripa Pave.

|| Doha ||
Brahma Chalisa Padhe, Jnan Bhakti Man Laay.
Srijanshakti Buddhi Badhe, Sab Vidya Phal Paay.
hindiMeaning:
सृजनहार ब्रह्मा देव, कमलासन भगवान, भक्ति से चालीसा कहता हूँ, ज्ञान का वरदान दो। चार मुख, चार वेदों के ज्ञाता, परम प्रवीण ब्रह्मा जी की हृदय से वन्दना करता हूँ।

विष्णु की नाभि से कमल पर प्रकट हुए, चार मुखों से चार वेद (ऋग्, यजुर्, साम, अथर्व) प्रकट किये। सावित्री-सरस्वती आपकी शक्ति हैं। हंसवाहन, श्वेत वस्त्र, कमण्डलु, रुद्राक्षमाला, वेदपुस्तक धारी।

सृष्टि के आदि प्रजापति, चौदह भुवन के रचयिता। सप्तर्षि (मरीचि, अत्रि, अंगिरा, पुलस्त्य, पुलह, क्रतु) और दक्ष प्रजापति आपके पुत्र हैं। मनु-शतरूपा मानसपुत्र हैं। युग, कल्प, मन्वन्तर की कालरचना आपकी कृति है।

ब्रह्मलोक (सत्यलोक) सबसे ऊँचा धाम है। त्रिमूर्ति में प्रथम, पुष्कर में एकमात्र मन्दिर है। गायत्री मन्त्र आपकी महान शक्ति है।

जो नित्य इस चालीसा का पाठ करता है, उसे सृजनशक्ति, बुद्धि और सब विद्या का फल प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Invokes Lord Brahma, the creator of the universe
Invokes creativity, innovation, and artistic abilities
Invokes the blessing of knowledge of vedas and sacred scriptures
Promotes success in education and intellectual pursuits
Unique prayer as Brahma temples are extremely rare
Powerful during Pushkar visit and Kartik Purnima
Invokes the blessings of saraswati and gayatri alongside brahma
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Brahma Chalisa — ब्रह्मा चालीसा — is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn offered to Lord Brahma. Rooted in Traditional Chalisa Sangrah, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant creativity, innovation, and artistic abilities as well as grant the blessings of Saraswati and Gayatri alongside Brahma. Additionally, it is known to promote success in education and intellectual pursuits. This sacred text is ideally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Vasant Panchami and Guru Purnima for enhanced blessings. At 42 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 15 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Brahma Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Brahma Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Prayer to Lord Brahma for creativity, knowledge, and wisdom.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Brahma Chalisa and which deity does it praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Brahma Chalisa is a 40-verse Hindi devotional composition (chalis = forty in Hindi, hence Chalisa) in the chaupai metre, praising Lord Brahma — the first member of the Trimurti and the cosmic creator (Srijanhar). The Chalisa opens with two dohas saluting Kamalasan Bhagwan (the lotus-throned Lord) with four faces and four Vedas, and the chaupais narrate his emergence from Vishnu's navel-lotus, his creation of the fourteen bhuvanas, his seven manas-putras (Marichi, Atri, Angiras, Pulastya, Pulaha, Kratu, Daksha), the manifestation of the four Vedas from his four mouths, his consorts Savitri and Saraswati, and his unique temple at Pushkar. (Sources: Traditional Chalisa Sangrah; Pushkar Brahma Mandir guides; Hindupedia Brahma.)

seoFaqs.question: Why are Brahma temples extremely rare in India?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Brahma has very few temples in India — the most famous being the Pushkar Brahma Mandir in Rajasthan — because of the curse narrated in the Padma Purana and Skanda Purana. According to the legend, Brahma performed a yajna at Pushkar without his wife Savitri (who was delayed), and married a milkmaid Gayatri to complete the ritual. The angered Savitri cursed Brahma that he would not be widely worshipped on earth despite being the creator. A second tradition narrates Brahma being cursed by Shiva for falsely claiming to have seen the top of the infinite Jyotirlinga (Lingodbhava story). Hence the Pushkar temple remains his unique pan-Indian shrine. (Sources: Padma Purana Srishti Khanda; Skanda Purana; Pushkar sthala-purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the four faces and four Vedas of Brahma?
seoFaqs.answer: Brahma is depicted with four faces (chatur-mukha) facing the four cardinal directions — east, south, west, north — symbolising his omniscient gaze over all of creation. From each face emerged one of the four Vedas: the eastern face uttered the Rig Veda, the southern face the Yajur Veda, the western face the Sama Veda, and the northern face the Atharva Veda. The Chalisa explicitly states 'Char mukh hain tumhre suvishala, Char Ved mukh se nikle mala'. He also has four arms holding rudraksha-mala, kamandalu (water-pot), Veda-pustaka (sacred book), and showing varada-mudra. He sits on a hamsa (swan) and wears white garments. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana; Hindupedia Brahma; Wikipedia Brahma.)

seoFaqs.question: When should Brahma Chalisa be recited for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) are the prescribed weekdays for Brahma worship. The supreme occasion is Kartik Purnima — Brahma Jayanti — when the famous Pushkar Mela (Pushkar Camel Fair) is held and lakhs of pilgrims bathe in Pushkar Lake at sunrise. Other powerful days are Vasant Panchami (since Saraswati, Brahma's consort, is invoked), the Magha and Kartik months, Madhyahna (noon when Brahma is most active), and brahma-muhurta (pre-dawn, named after Brahma himself). Students, scholars, writers, scientists, architects and creative professionals chant the Chalisa to invoke srijan-shakti (creative power) and buddhi (intellect). (Sources: Drikpanchang Pushkar Mela; Pushkar Devasthan Trust; Vedic calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Brahma Chalisa grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The closing doha promises that whoever recites the Chalisa with devotion gains srijan-shakti (creative power), buddhi (sharp intellect), and the fruit of all vidyas (sab vidya phal paay). Tradition extends this to mastery of the Vedas and shastras, success in education and intellectual pursuits, breakthrough creative ideas for artists/writers/scientists/architects, blessings for safe childbirth and good progeny (since Brahma is Prajapati, the lord of progeny), removal of obstacles in research and academic projects, and finally Brahmaloka (Satyaloka) — the highest of the seven upper worlds and the abode of the creator. (Sources: Traditional Chalisa Sangrah phalashruti; Hindupedia; Pushkar temple guides.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Brahma, Savitri and Gayatri?
seoFaqs.answer: Savitri is Brahma's first consort — the personification of the Savitur (Sun) and the goddess of sandhya-vandana. Gayatri is the meter and the deified mantra (Tat savitur varenyam) revealed by Brahma, often considered Brahma's second consort and the Veda-mata (mother of the Vedas). Saraswati, the goddess of knowledge, is also Brahma's consort and emerged from his manas. The Chalisa names all three: Savitri Shakti tav patni pyari, Saraswati vidya ki adhikari, Gayatri mantra tav shakti mahana. The three together represent Brahma's creative powers — Savitri the solar life-force, Saraswati pure knowledge, Gayatri the sound-form of Brahman. (Sources: Devi Bhagavata Purana; Hindupedia Savitri-Gayatri; Pushkar sthala-purana.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Brahma Chalisa differ from Brahma Stotram and Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: All three relate to Brahma but operate at different levels. The Brahma Chalisa is a 40-verse Hindi praise composition for daily devotional recitation, accessible to anyone. The Brahma Stotram refers to various Sanskrit hymns (such as the Brahma Sahasranama from Padma Purana, the Brahma Stuti from Bhagavata Skandha 10) used by Sanskrit-trained pandits during yajnas. The Gayatri Mantra (Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah Tat Savitur Varenyam) is the supreme Vedic mantra revealed by Brahma to the rishis — it is the mantra of Brahma rather than to Brahma, and is recited during sandhya-vandana three times daily by every dvija (twice-born). The Chalisa is the popular bhakti vehicle, the stotras the ritual vehicle, the Gayatri the meditative vehicle. (Sources: Hindupedia; Pandit Shriram Sharma Acharya Gayatri literature.)
